requirements: Subsystem module: Dorsal Control Module x 2

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value
[power_signal_separation] good: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry.
[return_path_and_grounding_intent] good: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals.
[board_mounting_and_clearance] good: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns.
Each required evidence_feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops
Adjacent interfaces must be named and reciprocal between subsystems
Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent

risk.desc: Tool-less lid design may introduce complexity in manufacturing and assembly.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough prototyping and testing phases to ensure ease of manufacturing and assembly.

risk.desc: Wireless board bay might require additional RF shielding to prevent interference.
risk.mit: Include RF shielding materials and conduct EMC tests during development.

risk.desc: Service label and cable exits could be prone to wear and tear over time.
risk.mit: Use durable materials for labels and protective coatings for cables.

requirements: Supports force feedback from actuator to knuckle mount

Heat dissipation from actuator to knuckle mount
Removable battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges

Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access
Flexion-resistance actuator modules plug in and remain visible near knuckle row
Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance
Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles
